channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Building materials dumped on public green, 3rd July
item.description:
Building materials have dumped on the public green widely used by residents and children. The is unacceptable, dangerous and will set a dangerous precedent for builders and merchants if they think they can leave materials anywhere they please without consequence. Please action immediately!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hillcrest Road, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car in car park, 3rd July
item.description:
This car has been here for about a month - it is parked quite badly and prevents others from parking in these spaces. I was given a ticket as i was parked in my own private car park but outside of a space due to none being available.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): New North Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned trailer, 3rd July
item.description:
Recovery trailer in betts way for the last 10 plus weeks taking up at least 3 parking spaces it's a cul de sac not enough parking as it is
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Betts Way, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pond needs clearing h desperately in Priory Gardens, 3rd July
item.description:
Saw men clearing it today not completed a good job of it - still terrible and smells awful thx

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Street, Ramsden, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Platts Lane, adjacent to the corner property at 2 Hollycroft Avenue, London NW3., 3rd July
item.description:
Overgrown and low hanging foliage making it impossible to walk on the pavement, leading to pedestrians having to go on to the road. The hedge and tree branches from the property at the corner of 2 Hollycroft Avenue and Platts Lane have become significantly overgrown and are overhanging the public footway on Platts Lane. The vegetation extends well beyond the property's boundary, creating a head-height obstruction. Pedestrians, are forced to duck beneath the branches or move to the road to pass. This creates an unnecessary hazard for wheelchair users, people with mobility impairments, parents with pushchairs, and those with visual impairments. The overgrowth also reduces visibility along the pavement and, if left unattended, is likely to become a greater obstruction as the vegetation continues to grow. I would be grateful if Camden Council could inspect the site and require the property owner to cut back the hedge and tree branches so that the footway is restored to a safe and fully accessible width in accordance with the Council's responsibilities for maintaining unobstructed public highways. I have attached a photograph showing the extent of the overhanging vegetation.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Platt's Lane, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bollards etc need removing from Edmund Road, 3rd July
item.description:
Works have been completed these bollards and yellow builders fence need removing from Edmund Road many Thx sorry I know it’s the wrong category but didn’t know which one to put this under

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Edmund Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Household rubbish in Tilbury Close, 3rd July
item.description:
Rubbish from entrance to Tilbury Close to approximately lane leading to garages on right hand side.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tilbury Close, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London

item.title: A Heap Of Tree Cutting Dumped, 3rd July
item.description:
Someone has had some trees cut down in back garden by either council or private company and they have just dumped the tree cuttings
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sandringham Road, Sundridge, Greater London

item.title: Rubbish all across the pavements and streets, 3rd July
item.description:
It’s dirty here all the time - need more regular cleans. People are fly tipping constantly and it’s generally disgusting.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pembury Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing paving stones after tree removal-trip hazard, 3rd July
item.description:
Outside 5 Selborne Road, Sidcup …a large verge tree was removed from here and paving is needed to remake the pavement line. There is evidence of bright green paint by surveyors but it still needs doing. Estimate two slabs or tarmac as so often done. Children, pushchairs, seniors and wheelchairs from the care home in The Drive use this street daily. Needs to be put right asap.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Selborne Road, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drain needs a clean, 3rd July
item.description:
Flood water breached porch of 144 in recent heavy rain. Drains need a clean. The drains are in the middle of a disabled bay with a car permanently parked here, so likely drain hasn’t been accessed. If you do attend to clean and red car is parked in the way, please knock at number 140.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen Anne Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass and rubbish, 3rd July
item.description:
Health hazard

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Park Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Enormous fly-posting, 3rd July
item.description:
Huge correx fly-posting advert just been put up

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Canberra Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous Junction, 3rd July
item.description:
The M11 northbound exit for Loughton leads onto Chigwell Lane, IG10, and is extremely dangerous. Cars exit this junction and in order to continue to Chigwell, they need pull out to a central reservation that accommodates one vehicle. They then need to pull out onto two lane oncoming traffic. There have been numerous accidents at this 'black spot', with the most recent being on 02/07/26 resulting in serious damage to numerous vehicles and multiple injuries. I would suggest that this manoeuvre is prohibited with adequate signage, and a roundabout is facilitated to enable drivers to safely travel east to Chigwell Lane.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish bag dumped, 3rd July
item.description:
This was dumped outside my house after the bins had been emptied, unfortunately I didn't see them do it

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): West Hallowes, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: General waste bin left unattended for 4 months, 3rd July
item.description:
Waste bin has been sitting here on the corner of Matham Grove/East Dulwich Grove for 4 months. Several reports have been made and it is still not been sorted

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 26, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London

Graffiti tagging on bus driver’s convenience.

item.title: Electrical appliances dumped, 3rd July
item.description:
Rubbish and household appliance left on street.

item.title: Hole at he Halfway Street end of Corbylands Road near the bend., 3rd July
item.description:
The hole has been repaired before but a deep hole has reappeared and getting deeper.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Corbylands Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Male entered private residents block and dumped building waste/wood down rubbish chute, 3rd July
item.description:
I am a resident within Flats at 15 Brent Road/Santana SE18 3DP. I’m conscious in the past few months that there had been a repeat issue of building/DIY rubbish left in the communal bin area and we were notified by our management company that residents would have to foot the cost of removal of these items if a persistent issue. Of note a few flats on the higher floors have had decorators/contractors in past 2 months. On Monday 29th June approx 09:15 am I observed a male pull up outside in his van (see photo grey/silver van with damaged right wing mirror, sign written: JCP Property Services 07950546513 mob 02083045886 office), He approached the block holding offcuts of wooden decking and other building waste and attempted to get into the residents bin store. Having failed that he has buzzed one of the flats and been allowed access to the communal hallway, he has walked up the stairs (2nd or 3rd floor - Flats 7-14) and then thrown these items down the rubbish chute (this has been heard), he has then immediately left the premises. Following this, I have checked the bins and found building waste and wood blocking the chute (see photos.) Which I have now removed, also notified management company of this. The waste consisted of off cuts of wooden decking ( the block doesn't have a decking area) and a bundle of tiles tied up. This man is not a resident within the block. I would describe the male as aged 45-50 years old, 5'8-5'10, white but quite tanned, slim build, short light brown hair wearing a lemon yellow polo top, he appeared to have droopy eyes.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brent Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet
